reasoning: A binding treaty with 35+ state ratifications requires multiple stages of diplomatic negotiation: drafting, adoption, signing, and domestic ratification. This sequence historically takes 5-10 years. As of May 2025, not to mention having 35+ ratifications, there is not even a formally adopted or drafted treaty text containing the 5 stringent bans (deployment, experiments, public funding, patents, and working against it internationally). The "Solar Geoengineering Non-Use Agreement" remains an advocacy campaign led by scientists and supported by an open letter. While some countries at the UN Environment Assembly have voiced support for non-use, there is no active intergovernmental treaty mechanism on course to be ratified by 35 countries before the end of 2025. Reaching this threshold within merely seven months is virtually impossible. I am setting the probability to the lowest allowed threshold specified in the rules.
